metadata.tool_description:
Get dataset metadata by ID or UUID.

Returns columns, metrics, and schema details.

IMPORTANT FOR LLM CLIENTS:
- Use numeric ID (e.g., 123) or UUID string (e.g., "a1b2c3d4-...")
- DO NOT use schema.table_name format (e.g., "public.customers")
- To find a dataset ID, use the list_datasets tool first

Example usage:
```json
{
    "identifier": 123
}
```

Or with UUID:
```json
{
    "identifier": "a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-1234567890ab"
}
```

GetDatasetInfoRequest.description: Request schema for get_dataset_info with support for ID or UUID.

use_cache.description: Whether to use Superset's cache layers. When True, will serve from cache if available (query results, metadata, form data). When False, will bypass cache and fetch fresh data.
use_cache.title: Use Cache

force_refresh.description: Whether to force refresh cached data. When True, will invalidate existing cache entries and fetch fresh data, then update the cache. Overrides use_cache=True if both are specified.
force_refresh.title: Force Refresh

refresh_metadata.description: Whether to refresh metadata cache for datasets, tables, and columns. Useful when database schema has changed and you need fresh metadata.
refresh_metadata.title: Refresh Metadata

identifier.description: Dataset identifier - can be numeric ID or UUID string
identifier.title: Identifier

GetDatasetInfoRequest.title: GetDatasetInfoRequest

llm_calls.response:
```yaml
version: "1.0"
tests:
  - name: test_get_dataset_info_by_numeric_id
    prompt: "Can you get the detailed information for dataset ID 42? I need to see the columns, metrics, and schema details."
    evaluators:
      - name: execution_successful
      - name: was_mcp_tool_called
        args:
          tool_name: "get_dataset_info"
  - name: test_get_dataset_info_by_uuid
    prompt: "I have this dataset UUID a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-1234567890ab and need to check its metadata including all available columns and any defined metrics."
    evaluators:
      - name: execution_successful
      - name: was_mcp_tool_called
        args:
          tool_name: "get_dataset_info"
```

IMPORTANT FOR LL...

test_scenarios.name: valid_numeric_id
test_scenarios.description: Test with valid numeric dataset ID to retrieve dataset metadata

test_scenarios.name: valid_uuid_string
test_scenarios.description: Test with valid UUID string identifier to retrieve dataset metadata

test_scenarios.name: nonexistent_numeric_id
test_scenarios.description: Test with numeric ID that doesn't exist in the system

test_scenarios.name: nonexistent_uuid
test_scenarios.description: Test with UUID that doesn't exist in the system

test_scenarios.name: invalid_identifier_format
test_scenarios.description: Test with invalid identifier formats (e.g., schema.table_name which is explicitly not supported)

test_scenarios.name: cache_behavior_enabled
test_scenarios.description: Test with use_cache=true to verify cached data is returned when available

test_scenarios.name: cache_behavior_disabled
test_scenarios.description: Test with use_cache=false to verify fresh data is fetched

test_scenarios.name: force_refresh_behavior
test_scenarios.description: Test with force_refresh=true to verify cache invalidation and fresh data fetch

test_scenarios.name: metadata_refresh
test_scenarios.description: Test with refresh_metadata=true to verify schema metadata is refreshed

test_scenarios.name: empty_or_null_identifier
test_scenarios.description: Test with empty string, null, or missing identifier

test_scenarios.name: malformed_uuid
test_scenarios.description: Test with malformed UUID strings (wrong format, invalid characters)

test_scenarios.name: zero_numeric_id
test_scenarios.description: Test with zero as numeric ID

test_scenarios.name: very_large_numeric_id
test_scenarios.description: Test with extremely large numeric IDs (boundary testing)

test_scenarios.name: conflicting_cache_parameters
test_scenarios.description: Test with conflicting cache parameters (use_cache=true, force_refresh=true)

validation_points: Column data types are properly formatted
Error messages are descriptive for invalid inputs
Response time is reasonable for cache vs non-cache requests
No sensitive information is exposed in responses
Response format is consistent across identifier types
Proper HTTP status codes for different scenarios
